no_skill_detail.why: The answer uses only a standard begin/insert/commit pattern and never calls conn.fence(), which the task specifies is required for durability.

with_skill_detail.why: The code correctly calls conn.fence() immediately before conn.commit() and explicitly explains that omitting fence() would silently drop the transaction.

no_skill_detail.why: The answer neither references limiter.remaining nor explains window handling, instead using wait/await_wait per call, so it doesn’t clearly follow or contradict the specified correct approach.

with_skill_detail.why: The answer explicitly warns against polling limiter.remaining in the loop and instead shows code that reads it once and tracks the count locally per window.

no_skill_detail.why: The answer refuses to use flazon at all and does not recommend either plain dump/load or ordered(), instead asking for clarification.

with_skill_detail.why: The answer explicitly recommends flazon.dump(flazon.ordered(users)) as the main solution and contrasts it against the plain dump/load trap.
